subject: [PATCH 2/6] nfsd: release path refs on follow_down() error

reviews.author: Al Viro
reviews.summary: Identified a potential issue with untested code in nfsd_cross_mnt() and suggested a cleanup to move path_put() calls after the point where codepaths join.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton
reviews.summary: Acknowledged Al's feedback and expressed willingness to test the patch.
reviews.raw_body:
On Mon, 2026-06-01 at 20:31 +0100, Al Viro wrote:
> On Mon, Jun 01, 2026 at 02:57:16PM -0400, Jeff Layton wrote:
> 
> > Looks reasonable. Chuck has already taken this patch into his tree, but
> > we could do a cleanup on top. Want to send us an "official" patch?
> 
> Not a problem, but it's completely untested.  It compiles, but...
>

Thanks Al. I'll toss it onto the pile that I'm testing.
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: Indicated that the patch can be replaced by any of the patches already in nfsd-testing, and suggested it as a topic branch.

2026-06-01.patch_summary: Patch to release path refs on follow_down() error in nfsd_cross_mnt() function, with a suggested cleanup of untested code.
